rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF FRANCES FISCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2021. 1) Please file Trustee timesheets that conform to LRSF 14.53. Time records must identify the billing party providing the service and include the hourly rate for each billing party, the time spent on the services described in the entry, and the total fee charged for that entry. 2) There is no verification of the balance of assets in the IRA account on 3/15/2021. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 3) Please file evidence showing the beneficiary of the IRA policy. Please file a receipt from the beneficiary showing they received distribution. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/7/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY JEAN DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Probate Code Section 10520(c) limits cash to general pecuniary devisees to $10,000 to any one person. The executor (not the administrator) distributed the gifts without a Court order. The narrative listing of attorney costs in paragraph 21 of the amended petition, in addition to being challenging to read, does not set forth the dates such costs were incurred so that the sometimes vague descriptions referring to "Notice" or submitting filed pleadings, etc., are not adequately supported and/or appear to include duplicates. Additionally, there are references to a confirmation of sale, but the register of actions does not reflect any court confirmed sale. Given the amendment reducing fees, how does the rest of the petition remain unchanged? (See paragraph 5 of the amendment filed on 7/7/21.) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if a BENEFICIARY is or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc., not just heirs. No claims were filed for the fees incurred in the conservatorship (which case was not referenced in this petition) and paid by the executor? Why was there a redemption of RiverSource Flexible Annuity on 6/30/20? This asset was not on any I&A. (The executor was appointed on 6/26/2019; the order was not FILED until 6/27/19. The JC form GC-400(SUM) is not appropriate for decedents' estates.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner by 9/2/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTYE M. MCNAULTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the amendment did not, in fact, amend the math in paragraph 22 of the petition to show the correct balance of costs to be reimbursed to the administrator. (The filing fee for an initial petition for probate IS $450. The filing fee for a petition for final distribution - such as THIS petition - is $435.) (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 5, 1985 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Return Of Trust Property, To Confirm That Settlors Financial Accounts Are Property Of The Trust; For Insturctions; For Removal Of Co-Trus
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Brian Williams's Petition a) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.60 E.] b) The proof of service filed on 6/22/21 (no courtesy copy has been provided [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.) shows that notice was mailed to Vivian Williams in care of her attorney only [CRC 7.51(a).], and item 5 was not checked, so a copy of the petition was apparently not served along with the notice [Prob. C. Section 851]. c) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] The address of the attorney representing a party is not the last known address of an interested party. d) Neither the petition nor the (incorrect) form of notice identifies the property at issue. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/2/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 5, 1985 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition (1) To Invalidate First Trust Amendment To 2008 Restatement And Amendment To Williams Family Trust; (2) To Invalidate Improper Transfers To Co-Trustee Due To Undue Influence And Elder Abuse; (3) Invalidate Transfers To And Actions Of Respondent In Capacity As Attorney In Fact; (4) To Invalidate Transfers To Respondent Co Trustee As Self Dealing And Elder Abuse; (5) For Constructive Trust; (6) For Recovery Of Trust Property And Accounting; (7) For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (8) For Removal Of Respondent
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Vivian Williams's Petition: a) No proof of service of the proper form of Notice of Hearing has been filed yet. b) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] The address of the attorney representing a party is not the last known address of an interested party. c) Notice must be mailed individually and directly to the person entitled to notice. If the person is represented by an attorney, the notice must also be sent to the attorney but not only to the attorney unless the party entitled to notice has directed in writing that notice be sent only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51 and Probate Code Section 1214.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/2/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALESSANDRO M. BACCARI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/15/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. The will is a pour-over will. Item 8 of the petition must clearly identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, item 8 of the petition must clearly identify all of the trust beneficiaries. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/3/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
